differentials.name: Septum primum = thin partition dividing primitive atrium into right and left atria at 4th week; membrane grows from dorsal atrial wall toward endocardial cushion → ultimately forms thin flap valve of fossa ovalis
differentials.raw: Septum primum = thin partition dividing primitive atrium into right and left atria at 4th week; membrane grows from dorsal atrial wall toward endocardial cushion → ultimately forms thin flap valve of fossa ovalis

differentials.name: Ostium primum = temporary orifice between septum primum + endocardial cushions close to AV valves; it becomes obliterated by 5th week
differentials.raw: Ostium primum = temporary orifice between septum primum + endocardial cushions close to AV valves; it becomes obliterated by 5th week

differentials.name: Ostium secundum = multiple small coalescing fenestrations in center of septum primum
differentials.raw: Ostium secundum = multiple small coalescing fenestrations in center of septum primum

differentials.name: Septum secundum = stiff membrane developing on right side of septum primum growing from right atrial roof → ultimately forms thicker limbus of fossa ovalis
differentials.raw: Septum secundum = stiff membrane developing on right side of septum primum growing from right atrial roof → ultimately forms thicker limbus of fossa ovalis

differentials.name: Foramen ovale = orifice limited by septum secundum + septum primum
differentials.raw: Foramen ovale = orifice limited by septum secundum + septum primum

differentials.name: Foramen ovale flap = lower edge of septum primum (patent in 6%, probe-patent in 25%); not considered an ASD
differentials.raw: Foramen ovale flap = lower edge of septum primum (patent in 6%, probe-patent in 25%); not considered an ASD

context: Right Atrium (RA) Components: Embryology:   originates from primitive trabeculated RA (persists as RA appendage) + from sinus venosus forming smooth-walled portion of RA Right Atrial Appendage =  triangular superior extension of RA that wraps around aortic root √  pyramidal shape with narrow base Rx:  right atrial lead tip is typically placed at right atrial appendage for pacemaker / internal cardiac defibrillator Sinus Venosus ›  located in posterolateral wall of RA between orifices of SVC and IVC (a)  right horn: gives rise to crista terminalis, eustachian ridge, thebesian valve (b)  left ho
